prerequisites: A PandaDoc account. API keys are tied to the user who generates them, and per PandaDoc's docs only an Org Admin can generate an API key.
A PandaDoc API key (the required `api_key` config field). PandaDoc issues Sandbox keys directly through the Dev Center, while Production keys require approval from PandaDoc before use.
A replication start date for the required `start_date` config field, used to bound how far back the connector reads records.

authMethods.name: API key
authMethods.summary: PandaDoc authenticates this connector with the `api_key` secret field. The key is sent on every request in the header `Authorization: API-Key {api_key}`. Keys are generated in the PandaDoc Dev Center (Sandbox keys are self-served; Production keys require PandaDoc approval). Note: although the PandaDoc API also supports OAuth 2.0 Bearer authentication, this connector exposes only an `api_key` field and therefore uses API-key authentication.

setupSteps.title: Generate a PandaDoc API key
setupSteps.body: Sign in to PandaDoc as an Org Admin and open the Dev Center from the PandaDoc Developers portal (Access Dev Center). Generate an API key on the Dev Center configuration page. Use a Sandbox key for testing (issued immediately) or a Production key (requires approval from PandaDoc). Note that generating a new key invalidates the existing key of the same type, and keys deactivate if the owning user is removed from the workspace, so prefer a stable long-term user.

setupSteps.title: Provide the API key
setupSteps.body: Enter the generated key into the connector's required `api_key` field. The connector sends it as `Authorization: API-Key {api_key}` on each request. Keep the key secret; treat it as a credential.

setupSteps.title: Set the start date
setupSteps.body: Set the required `start_date` field to the earliest date from which the connector should replicate PandaDoc records. Data created before this date is not synced.
